_comment: Provider/cascade config. Edited by humans and by the model-watch bot PRs. structured: json_schema = strict structured outputs; json_object = schema embedded in prompt. Model IDs verified July 2026: openrouter cohere/north-mini-code:free and poolside/laguna-m.1:free confirmed live in catalog (qwen/qwen3-coder:free was removed and replaced); gemini-3.6-flash is the current live Flash model on Google's OpenAI-compatible endpoint (no google/ prefix) — gemini-2.0-flash still resolves as a valid model ID but AI Studio shows 0 RPM/TPM/RPD quota on it (deprecated for new free-tier allocation, existence-check alone doesn't catch this — see check_models.py note), confirmed dead via live 429s across three repos in one session, gemini-3.6-flash verified live with real quota, structured json_schema output, and vision. gemini-3.5-flash-lite added as a same-provider second tier (before groq/openrouter) — AI Studio shows it at 15 RPM/500 RPD vs gemini-3.6-flash's 5 RPM/20 RPD, so a Gemini daily-cap exhaustion falls over to a much larger quota on the same provider before dropping to a different one; verified live with structured json_schema output. groq llama-3.3-70b-versatile is the current Groq free-tier Llama 70B model. deepseek-v4-flash confirmed current on DeepSeek's own OpenAI-compatible API (api-docs.deepseek.com) — deepseek-chat/deepseek-reasoner deprecate 2026-07-24. `creative` cascade: for social-caption/article-generation style prompts (not code review) — openai/gpt-oss-120b:free and meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct:free (repos' original hardcoded picks) are both 404 dead on OpenRouter's free tier as of this check, and a reasoning-hybrid model tried as a replacement (nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b:free) leaked its raw chain-of-thought into the caption text under a real constrained prompt — google/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it:free and gemma-4-31b-it:free verified live across three different repos' prompt shapes (short captions, JSON categorization, 400-600 word structured articles): clean output, no reasoning leak, no markdown fence, correct JSON parse every time.
